findings.title: Passwords use unsalted fast SHA-256 hashing
Password credential comparison is not constant-time
Reset tokens are not consumed after successful verification
findings.quote: if (!record || record.used || record.expiresAt < Date.now()) return false;

reasoning: All repository files were inspected. The reset route limiter is mounted before its route, reset tokens use randomBytes(32), reset errors are generic, and no development bypass appears. However, password storage is a fast unsalted SHA-256 hash, its comparison is not constant-time, and reset-token verification never marks a valid token as used.
